message:
fix(io/copy): require shutdown completion before declaring bidirectional copy done

The bidirectional copy loop previously declared a direction "done" once
read_done was true and the buffer was fully flushed (pos >= cap). This
missed the case where shutdown of the write half is still in progress —
the copy would return Ok before the peer had received the FIN, potentially
causing data loss or RST on the remote side.

Now, the completion check requires all three conditions:
  - read_done: the read half has returned EOF
  - pos >= cap: all buffered data has been written
  - shutdown_done: AsyncWrite::poll_shutdown returned Ready(Ok(()))

A new DeferredShutdownDuplex test helper returns Pending on the first
shutdown poll to simulate slow shutdown (e.g., TLS close_notify handshake).

Test: copy_bidirectional_waits_for_shutdown_completion creates two
DeferredShutdownDuplex streams, writes one byte each direction, and
verifies both directions transfer correctly even when shutdown is deferred.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
